title: Bargabhima temple
tagline: Bargabhima temple — spiritual town of West Bengal

overview.short: Bargabhima Temple is a Hindu ancient temple situated at Tamluk in Purba Medinipur district of West Bengal.
overview.description: Bargabhima Temple is a Hindu ancient temple situated at Tamluk in Purba Medinipur district of West Bengal. The Bargabhima Temple, also known as Vibhash Shakti Peeth and Bhimakali Mandir, is devoted to the goddess Sati. This particular shrine of one of the 51 Shaktipeeth Temples stands by River Roopnarayana in Tamluk village, Purba Medinipur district in West Bengal. It was constructed by the Tamluk Raj family.

topPlaces.name: Tamluk
topPlaces.description: Tamluk (Bengali pronunciation: [tɔmluk]), is a city and a municipality in the Indian state of West Bengal. It is one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities of the world and the headquarters of the Purba Medinipur district.

topPlaces.name: Chak Srikrishnapur
topPlaces.description: Chak Srikrishnapur is a village in Tamluk CD block in Tamluk subdivision of Purba Medinipur district in the state of West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Chanmari Ground
topPlaces.description: Chanmari Ground is a multi-purpose sports ground located in Kurseong, West Bengal, India. The ground was built in the early 1960s.

topPlaces.name: Tamluk (community development block)
topPlaces.description: Tamluk is a community development block that forms an administrative division in Tamluk subdivision of Purba Medinipur district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Janu Basan
topPlaces.description: Janu Basan (also written as Janubasan) is a village in Sahid Matangini CD block in Tamluk subdivision of Purba Medinipur district in the state of West Bengal, India.

items.note: Tamluk (Bengali pronunciation: [tɔmluk]), is a city and a municipality in the Indian state of West Bengal. It is one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities of the world and the headquarters of the Purba Medinipur district.
Chak Srikrishnapur is a village in Tamluk CD block in Tamluk subdivision of Purba Medinipur district in the state of West Bengal, India.
Chanmari Ground is a multi-purpose sports ground located in Kurseong, West Bengal, India. The ground was built in the early 1960s.
Tamluk is a community development block that forms an administrative division in Tamluk subdivision of Purba Medinipur district in the Indian state of West Bengal.
Janu Basan (also written as Janubasan) is a village in Sahid Matangini CD block in Tamluk subdivision of Purba Medinipur district in the state of West Bengal, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bargabhima temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bargabhima temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bargabhima temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport, Kolkata (67 km) and the nearest railway station is Howrah Junction (Kolkata) (54 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bargabhima temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bargabhima temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bargabhima temple?
faq.a: Stays in Bargabhima temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bargabhima temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Tamluk, Chak Srikrishnapur, Chanmari Ground, Tamluk (community development block), Janu Basan.
